rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. ANIYAH KEITH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 4, 2024 line 8. PLAINTIFF EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT continued to December 13, 2024 for Plaintiff's counsel to address why an order to show cause shall not issue for Plaintiff's failure to comply with the Court's minute Order twice. Plaintiff may file a response no later than December 9, 2024 explaining why Plaintiff failed to (1) file and serve a supplemental brief limited to citations to cases where Stipulation for Entry of Judgment (not an actual judgment) was enforced pursuant to CCP 128. CCP 128 is the only code section cited in the "Court Has Authority to Enter Judgment" section of the moving brief or (2) If no such case authority is located, the brief shall state "no cases." If the explanation is satisfactory, the Court will not issue an order to show cause. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ALI A. AMINI ET AL VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Courts Order Granting Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 4, 2024 line 15. PLAINTIFF ALI AMINI , AN INDIVIDUAL, SORAYA TONKABONI-AMINI NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF COURTS ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT. DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to identify new or different facts, circumstances or law that would support reconsideration and modification, amendment or revocation of the 10/21/2024 Order. Plaintiffs miscomprehend the summary judgment order. Summary judgment was granted because Defendants Andrew Linton and Angelina Mailani Greep made a prima facie showing that the notice overstated the rent due by presenting evidence that Plaintiffs owed them a rent credit of $57.61 and, as demonstrated by their online ledger (offered without objection), Plaintiffs failed to credit Defendants account any point in time. Plaintiffs failed to present any cognizable evidence otherwise. Thus, the only reasonable conclusion (without relying on wholesale conjecture) is the notice overstated rent due by at least $57.61, i.e., the amount of the credit. Whether Plaintiffs could ever recover rent due for April through September 2023 is immaterial to the summary judgment ruling. Defendants' request for sanctions is DENIED. Notably, Defendants seek sanctions under CCP 128.7, but they failed to comply with the requirements of that section, including the safe harbor provisions. (See CCP 128.7(c)(1).) =(501/JMQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ANDREW A LY VS. ELENA GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 4, 2024 line 11. DEFENDANT ELENA GARCIA DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The operative complaint alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on a Notice to Quit asserting just cause for eviction under SFRO. The operative complaint expressly alleges applicability and compliance with SFRO. Defendant failed to cite any authority for the proposition that reference to TPA 2019 in the complaint and in the operative notice render an eviction otherwise compliant with SFRO a sham or uncertain. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
